Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search QuickBooks for all open bills to discover booked contingent liabilities and accruals using quickbooks_search_bills. Find: (a) bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold (DocNumber HM-2026-Q1-0042-HOLD), Heartland Movers Q1 invoice, $12,800 total, payment frozen, lines 1-5 verified ($8,000), lines 6-8 disputed ($4,800): Lily Marchetti cancelled, Danielle Osei cancelled, Priya Venkatesh completed by Swift not Heartland; (b) bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2 (DocNumber ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001), DOT/PHMSA penalty accrual, $50,000, due June 5, 2026, Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187, one confirmed documentation gap (Keiko Tanaka missing placarding certificate); (c) bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001 (DocNumber ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC), Mosaic prototype damage accrual, $90,000, vendor liability cap $50,000 per item, $40,000 MoveOps exposure, pending insurance adjudication; (d) bill_stipend_washington_keyboard_hold, Andre Washington (Mosaic) ergonomic keyboard $89, admin hold placed by Marcus 2026-03-15, duplicate receipt hash matches Kevin Tran; (e) bill_stipend_ekwueme_monitor_hold, Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop) monitor stand $45, admin hold placed by Marcus 2026-03-15, PDF template cluster flagged; (f) bill_stipend_tran_keyboard, Kevin Tran (Vectral) keyboard $89, already paid (Balance $0); (g) BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417 (DocNumber KM-44192-ICR), KeyMove Specialty Transport insurance claim rider, $1,200, for Emilia Cruz Steinway piano scratch during NorthWind stairwell extraction, TxnDate 2026-04-17.
OE 2: Search QuickBooks accounts for contingent liability balances using quickbooks_search_accounts. Find acct_contingent_liability_claims (CurrentBalance $90,000, Mosaic damage) and acct_dot_penalty_accrual (CurrentBalance $50,000, DOT penalties pending). These confirm the booked accrual amounts.
OE 3: Search QuickBooks invoices for Sunbelt and Palmetto entries using quickbooks_search_invoices. Find INV-2026-0398 ($4,800, Sunbelt Venture Partners) and INV-2026-0399 ($2,400, Palmetto Community Foundation, nonprofit rate). The QuickBooks amounts are correct, but the emailed PDF attachments were swapped on April 10: Sunbelt received the Palmetto nonprofit PDF and vice versa, exposing MoveOps' nonprofit pricing to a for-profit client.
OE 4: Search QuickBooks invoices for StormCloud entries using quickbooks_search_invoices (or quickbooks_search_customers for StormCloud). Find inv_stormcloud_01 ($26,400 premium package). Cross-reference with email and Slack to discover a $500 goodwill credit was applied to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319, a real accounting error confirmed by Marcus in email (email_email_ed395722f1d4).
OE 5: Search emails for Heartland Movers correspondence using search_emails (query: "Heartland" or "Jake Loomis" or "HM-2026-Q1-0042"). Find: (a) Jake Loomis's Q1 invoice email, $12,800 for 8 moves at $1,600 each; (b) Chloe's cancellation emails to Jake for Lily Marchetti (Feb 10) and Danielle Osei (Feb 13), Jake never acknowledged either; (c) Marcus's formal dispute letter requesting a corrected invoice for $8,000; (d) Jake's formal rate increase notice, Heartland implementing 20% rate increase effective July 1, 2026, explicitly tying it to the unresolved HM-2026-Q1-0042 dispute and stating Heartland leadership will not finalize new rates until the dispute is settled.
OE 6: Search emails for DOT/PHMSA compliance correspondence using search_emails (query: "DOT" or "PHMSA" or "Linda Castellano" or "hazmat compliance"). Find: (a) Linda Castellano's formal inquiry letter (Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187, penalties up to $59,017 per violation per day under 49 CFR 107.329); (b) Marcus's forward to Chloe requesting immediate hazmat audit (April 21); (c) Marcus's extension request to DOT (April 22); (d) Castellano's reply granting 15-day extension, revised deadline June 5, 2026, requiring signed originals or certified copies.
OE 7: Search emails for Swift Relocations rate increase using search_emails (query: "Greg Pallone" or "Swift pricing" or "surcharge"). Find Greg Pallone's email (email_email_9a6a5e8dc032) to Chloe: Swift implementing 15% base rate increase plus $500 DOT documentation surcharge per shipment, effective immediately (April 11, 2026), citing retroactive placarding certs and chain-of-custody compliance work.
OE 8: Search emails for Marcus's vendor strategy memo using search_emails (query: "vendor strategy memo" or "vendor consolidation"). Find Marcus's email (CC Elena) consolidating: (1) Heartland Q3 rate increase tied to HM-2026-Q1-0042 dispute, (2) Swift immediate pricing change (15% + $500 surcharge), (3) Hana's broader Heartland reconciliation gaps beyond the single invoice, (4) backup capacity assessment with Lakeline limited to Austin/Houston only.
OE 9: Search emails for Mosaic prototype damage claim details using search_emails (query: "Mosaic damage" or "prototype" or "Zara Okoye"). Find Emeka's formal incident report to Tamara Byrd and correspondence about the $90,000 damaged robotic arm (Unit #1), $50,000 vendor liability cap, $40,000 MoveOps exposure, and pending insurance adjudication.
OE 10: Search emails for StormCloud $500 credit misposting details using search_emails (query: "StormCloud" or "EST-2026-0287" or "goodwill credit"). Find Marcus's email confirming the $500 goodwill credit was applied to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319, and Julian's email providing original service recovery context.
OE 11: Search emails for BrightLoop pricing exposure using search_emails (query: "BrightLoop pricing" or "guardrails" or "white-glove"). Find Marcus's email (email_email_23e2c0b9f535) to Mina, Alejandro, David, and Chloe warning against ad hoc premium pricing without margin guardrails: no formalized international or white-glove pricing tiers exist, creating uncontrolled margin risk on BrightLoop's expansion requests.
OE 12: Search Slack in #finance (channel C005) for financial discussions using conversations_history (channel_id: C005) or conversations_search_messages (query: "accrual" or "exposure" or "Heartland" or "Mosaic" or "DOT"). Find: (a) Marcus's Heartland Q1 bill logging ($12,800, holding for Chloe's review, March 25); (b) Hana's Mosaic damage claim accounting entries (credit memo CM-2026-0415, $90K accrual); (c) discussion about DOT $50K penalty accrual; (d) Marcus's StormCloud $500 credit misposting confirmation (April 25).
OE 13: Search Airtable stipend records for flagged transactions using airtable_list_bases to find appMoveOpsOps001, then airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Stipend Transactions, field_name: Employee Name, values: "Kevin Tran" / "Andre Washington" / "Jordan Ekwueme"). Find: (a) rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, $89, Approval Status: Approved, Review Notes: Audit Flag Duplicate Hash; (b) rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, $89, Approval Status: Pending Approval, duplicate hash flagged; (c) rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand, $45, Approval Status: Pending Approval, PDF template match flagged.
OE 14: Search Linear for all financial exposure tracking tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "Heartland" or "dispute" or "DOT" or "stipend" or "Sunbelt" or "invoice" or "vendor strategy"). Find: (a) issue_heartland_q1_recon (#342, assignee Chloe Vance, state in_progress), Heartland Q1 vendor invoice reconciliation; (b) linear_issue_6f33a369d77e (#118, assignee Marcus Thorne), Heartland billing discrepancy consolidation; (c) linear_issue_cd35f1ab6130 (#417, assignee Marcus Thorne), Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice attachment exposure reconstruction; (d) issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (#220 / ENG-220, assignee Dmitri Volkov, state todo), receipt hash fingerprinting system; (e) linear_issue_d21d94ce9b36 (#318, assignee Marcus Thorne), CEO vendor strategy memo covering Heartland, Swift, and backup capacity; (f) DOT compliance project (project_dot_compliance_audit, lead Chloe, state started) with 5 sub-issues: Mei-Lin Zhao docs (#1, done), Keiko Tanaka docs (#2, in_progress), Raj Patel docs (#3, done), Joaquin Reyes docs (#4, done), Swift retroactive placarding cert (#5, todo).
OE 15: Look up email addresses for Elena Rostova, Hana Kim, and Alejandro Fuentes using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Elena Rostova", "Hana Kim", "Alejandro Fuentes"). Find elena.rostova@moveops.com, hana.kim@moveops.com, alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com.
OE 16: Update Heartland dispute Linear tickets with current consolidated findings using linear_create_comment on issue_heartland_q1_recon (#342) and/or linear_issue_6f33a369d77e (#118). Comment should note: Jake's 20% rate increase is now tied to the Q1 dispute resolution, Hana found broader reconciliation gaps beyond the original $4,800, all Heartland exposure is being consolidated for Elena's board-level liability review.
OE 17: Update DOT compliance Linear issues with current status using linear_create_comment on relevant issues (issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic #2 for Keiko Tanaka gap, and/or issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert #5 for the retroactive certificate). Comment should note: $50K accrual still booked, June 5 deadline holds, retroactive Swift certificate still outstanding, findings being rolled up into Elena's consolidated exposure report.
OE 18: Update Sunbelt/Palmetto Linear ticket (linear_issue_cd35f1ab6130 #417) with current status using linear_create_comment. Comment should note: QB invoices remain correct (INV-2026-0398 $4,800 / INV-2026-0399 $2,400), PDF swap exposure documented, corrected communications sent, inclusion in consolidated financial exposure report for Elena.
OE 19: Update stipend fraud Linear ticket (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection #220 / ENG-220) with current hold statuses using linear_create_comment. Comment should note: Kevin Tran STIP-2026-0089 $89 already paid (Balance $0 in QB), Andre Washington STIP-2026-0094 $89 and Jordan Ekwueme STIP-2026-0097 $45 remain on admin hold, total held exposure $134, pending HR verification from respective client contacts.
OE 20: Send email (via send_email) from marcus.thorne@moveops.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com, CC hana.kim@moveops.com and alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com, with the full consolidated financial exposure report. Email should cover every open item with booked amount, current status, and what has changed: (1) Heartland Q1 dispute, $12,800 on hold, $4,800 contested for 3 invalid moves, rate increase now tied to dispute resolution; (2) DOT penalty accrual, $50,000 booked, extension to June 5, one documentation gap (Keiko Tanaka missing cert); (3) Mosaic damage liability, $90,000 accrued, $50K insurance cap / $40K gap, pending adjudication; (4) Stipend fraud holds, $134 actively held (Andre $89 + Jordan $45), Kevin Tran $89 already paid; (5) Vendor rate increases, Heartland 20% effective July 1 (tied to dispute), Swift 15% + $500/shipment surcharge effective immediately; (6) Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap, pricing exposure from swapped PDFs; (7) StormCloud $500 credit, misapplied to cancelled estimate, needs correction; (8) KeyMove piano claim, $1,200 insurance rider for Steinway damage; (9) BrightLoop pricing gaps, no formalized international/white-glove tiers.
OE 21: Post a message in #finance (channel C005) using conversations_add_message summarizing the consolidated financial exposure breakdown: all open contingent liabilities, disputed amounts, vendor rate increase implications, and pending exposure items with current dollar amounts and statuses, so the finance team has visibility before Marcus schedules working sessions.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search QuickBooks for all open bills to discover booked contingent liabilities and accruals using quickbooks_search_bills. Find: (a) bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold (DocNumber HM-2026-Q1-0042-HOLD), Heartland Movers Q1 invoice, $12,800 total, payment frozen, lines 1-5 verified ($8,000), lines 6-8 disputed ($4,800): Lily Marchetti cancelled, Danielle Osei cancelled, Priya Venkatesh completed by Swift not Heartland; (b) bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2 (DocNumber ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001), DOT/PHMSA penalty accrual, $50,000, due June 5, 2026, Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187, one confirmed documentation gap (Keiko Tanaka missing placarding certificate); (c) bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001 (DocNumber ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC), Mosaic prototype damage accrual, $90,000, vendor liability cap $50,000 per item, $40,000 MoveOps exposure, pending insurance adjudication; (d) bill_stipend_washington_keyboard_hold, Andre Washington (Mosaic) ergonomic keyboard $89, admin hold placed by Marcus 2026-03-15, duplicate receipt hash matches Kevin Tran; (e) bill_stipend_ekwueme_monitor_hold, Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop) monitor stand $45, admin hold placed by Marcus 2026-03-15, PDF template cluster flagged; (f) bill_stipend_tran_keyboard, Kevin Tran (Vectral) keyboard $89, already paid (Balance $0); (g) BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417 (DocNumber KM-44192-ICR), KeyMove Specialty Transport insurance claim rider, $1,200, for Emilia Cruz Steinway piano scratch during NorthWind stairwell extraction, TxnDate 2026-04-17.
OE 2: Search QuickBooks accounts for contingent liability balances using quickbooks_search_accounts. Find acct_contingent_liability_claims (CurrentBalance $90,000, Mosaic damage) and acct_dot_penalty_accrual (CurrentBalance $50,000, DOT penalties pending). These confirm the booked accrual amounts.
OE 3: Search QuickBooks invoices for Sunbelt and Palmetto entries using quickbooks_search_invoices. Find INV-2026-0398 ($4,800, Sunbelt Venture Partners) and INV-2026-0399 ($2,400, Palmetto Community Foundation, nonprofit rate). The QuickBooks amounts are correct, but the emailed PDF attachments were swapped on April 10: Sunbelt received the Palmetto nonprofit PDF and vice versa, exposing MoveOps' nonprofit pricing to a for-profit client.
OE 4: Search QuickBooks invoices for StormCloud entries using quickbooks_search_invoices (or quickbooks_search_customers for StormCloud). Find inv_stormcloud_01 ($26,400 premium package). Cross-reference with email and Slack to discover a $500 goodwill credit was applied to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319, a real accounting error confirmed by Marcus in email (email_email_ed395722f1d4).
OE 5: Search emails for Heartland Movers correspondence using search_emails (query: "Heartland" or "Jake Loomis" or "HM-2026-Q1-0042"). Find: (a) Jake Loomis's Q1 invoice email, $12,800 for 8 moves at $1,600 each; (b) Chloe's cancellation emails to Jake for Lily Marchetti (Feb 10) and Danielle Osei (Feb 13), Jake never acknowledged either; (c) Marcus's formal dispute letter requesting a corrected invoice for $8,000; (d) Jake's formal rate increase notice, Heartland implementing 20% rate increase effective July 1, 2026, explicitly tying it to the unresolved HM-2026-Q1-0042 dispute and stating Heartland leadership will not finalize new rates until the dispute is settled.
OE 6: Search emails for DOT/PHMSA compliance correspondence using search_emails (query: "DOT" or "PHMSA" or "Linda Castellano" or "hazmat compliance"). Find: (a) Linda Castellano's formal inquiry letter (Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187, penalties up to $59,017 per violation per day under 49 CFR 107.329); (b) Marcus's forward to Chloe requesting immediate hazmat audit (April 21); (c) Marcus's extension request to DOT (April 22); (d) Castellano's reply granting 15-day extension, revised deadline June 5, 2026, requiring signed originals or certified copies.
OE 7: Search emails for Swift Relocations rate increase using search_emails (query: "Greg Pallone" or "Swift pricing" or "surcharge"). Find Greg Pallone's email (email_email_9a6a5e8dc032) to Chloe: Swift implementing 15% base rate increase plus $500 DOT documentation surcharge per shipment, effective immediately (April 11, 2026), citing retroactive placarding certs and chain-of-custody compliance work.
OE 8: Search emails for Marcus's vendor strategy memo using search_emails (query: "vendor strategy memo" or "vendor consolidation"). Find Marcus's email (CC Elena) consolidating: (1) Heartland Q3 rate increase tied to HM-2026-Q1-0042 dispute, (2) Swift immediate pricing change (15% + $500 surcharge), (3) Hana's broader Heartland reconciliation gaps beyond the single invoice, (4) backup capacity assessment with Lakeline limited to Austin/Houston only.
OE 9: Search emails for Mosaic prototype damage claim details using search_emails (query: "Mosaic damage" or "prototype" or "Zara Okoye"). OE 16: Update Heartland dispute Linear tickets with current consolidated findings using linear_create_comment on issue_heartland_q1_recon (#342) and/or linear_issue_6f33a369d77e (#118). Comment should note: Jake's 20% rate increase is now tied to the Q1 dispute resolution, Hana found broader reconciliation gaps beyond the original $4,800, all Heartland exposure is being consolidated for Elena's board-level liability review.
OE 17: Update DOT compliance Linear issues with current status using linear_create_comment on relevant issues (issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic #2 for Keiko Tanaka gap, and/or issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert #5 for the retroactive certificate). Comment should note: $50K accrual still booked, June 5 deadline holds, retroactive Swift certificate still outstanding, findings being rolled up into Elena's consolidated exposure report.
OE 18: Update Sunbelt/Palmetto Linear ticket (linear_issue_cd35f1ab6130 #417) with current status using linear_create_comment. Comment should note: QB invoices remain correct (INV-2026-0398 $4,800 / INV-2026-0399 $2,400), PDF swap exposure documented, corrected communications sent, inclusion in consolidated financial exposure report for Elena.
OE 19: Update stipend fraud Linear ticket (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection #220 / ENG-220) with current hold statuses using linear_create_comment. Comment should note: Kevin Tran STIP-2026-0089 $89 already paid (Balance $0 in QB), Andre Washington STIP-2026-0094 $89 and Jordan Ekwueme STIP-2026-0097 $45 remain on admin hold, total held exposure $134, pending HR verification from respective client contacts.
OE 20: Send email (via send_email) from marcus.thorne@moveops.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com, CC hana.kim@moveops.com and alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com, with the full consolidated financial exposure report. Email should cover every open item with booked amount, current status, and what has changed: (1) Heartland Q1 dispute, $12,800 on hold, $4,800 contested for 3 invalid moves, rate increase now tied to dispute resolution; (2) DOT penalty accrual, $50,000 booked, extension to June 5, one documentation gap (Keiko Tanaka missing cert); (3) Mosaic damage liability, $90,000 accrued, $50K insurance cap / $40K gap, pending adjudication; (4) Stipend fraud holds, $134 actively held (Andre $89 + Jordan $45), Kevin Tran $89 already paid; (5) Vendor rate increases, Heartland 20% effective July 1 (tied to dispute), Swift 15% + $500/shipment surcharge effective immediately; (6) Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap, pricing exposure from swapped PDFs; (7) StormCloud $500 credit, misapplied to cancelled estimate, needs correction; (8) KeyMove piano claim, $1,200 insurance rider for Steinway damage; (9) BrightLoop pricing gaps, no formalized international/white-glove tiers.
OE 21: Post a message in #finance (channel C005) using conversations_add_message summarizing the consolidated financial exposure breakdown: all open contingent liabilities, disputed amounts, vendor rate increase implications, and pending exposure items with current dollar amounts and statuses, so the finance team has visibility before Marcus schedules working sessions.

✓Run #2The agent executed quickbooks_create_bill with Amount 40000, DocNumber 'ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC-REV1', description noting it supersedes the original $90K accrual. The tool returned success with 'Bill created successfully' and TotalAmt: 40000.0.
—Run #3No justification
✗Run #4No quickbooks_create_bill or quickbooks_update_account calls appear in the trajectory. Despite marking 'Fix stale/wrong amounts in QuickBooks' as completed, the agent made no actual QuickBooks write operations to revise the Mosaic accrual from $90,000 to ~$40,000.
✓Run #5quickbooks_update_account was called with account_id 'acct_contingent_liability_claims' and patch CurrentBalance of 40000, revising the Mosaic accrual from $90,000 to $40,000. The tool returned successfully.
